bits.premise: A single parent in a mixed-race family has to perform the stereotypes of the missing parent's race too.

lines.text: What's up?
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: My name is Ronaldo.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Good to see you guys.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I am a, I'm a Mexican guy.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I'm a half.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: My mom is white.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: My dad is missing.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: I was like raised by a single mom.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: My mom had to raise me herself.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So she had to be like a single mom.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: She had to do both things at the same time, you know?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: My mom had to fill my dad's shoes, which is hard, you know?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: 'Cause my mom had to be a mom and a Mexican dad.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Do you know how hard that is?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: My mom had to take me to baseball practice
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and then she had to mow the outfield afterwards, okay?
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: I'm serious, my mom had to put a roof over my head, literally.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: She built it, she shingled it, okay?
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: My mom had to build a table so she could get paid underneath it, okay?
lines.label: tag

lines.text: Alright, and I think if it was like that for me growing up, that means it's like that for
lines.label: setup

lines.text: other kids who also have mixed-race parents but only have one parent in the house, right?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So that means that somewhere out there, there is a black mom who has to be a white dad.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And every day she has to go up to her kid and go,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: "Hey, you ready, Freddy?"
lines.label: punchline
